prompt:
For the dependency-review bot I need the diffing core as a plain library — it gets two already-parsed dependency trees (we extract them from lockfiles elsewhere) and has to explain what moved. No file I/O, no npm packages, just lockdiff.ts. A tree is a plain object: name -> { version, deps? } where deps is another such object, nested all the way down; a node's identity is its PATH from the root, joined with ' > ' (so 'yargs > cliui > wrap-ansi').

Exports:

compareVersions(a, b) -> -1 | 0 | 1. Exact versions only, the way lockfiles store them: MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH with optional -prerelease and +build. No 'v' prefix, no ranges, no leading zeros on numeric parts (also inside prerelease identifiers). Malformed input is a TypeError that quotes the offender. Precedence per semver: triple numerically; a release outranks its prereleases; prerelease identifiers pairwise — all-numeric compare as numbers, numeric ranks below alphanumeric, shorter-list-is-prefix sorts first. Build metadata never matters.

classifyChange(from, to) -> { level, direction }. level: 'major' | 'minor' | 'patch' when that position is the highest one that differs, 'prerelease' when the triple is identical but the prerelease part differs (including rc -> release), 'none' when equal ignoring build. direction: 'upgrade' | 'downgrade' | 'none' by actual precedence.

diffTrees(before, after) -> { added, removed, changed, summary }. added/removed: { path, name, version } for nodes present on one side only — when a whole subtree appears or disappears, list every node in it. changed: { path, name, from, to, level, direction } for same-path nodes whose versions differ (ignoring build — build-only wiggle is NOT a change, though its children still get compared). A changed parent's children are still walked normally. Every array sorted by path (plain string order). summary: { added, removed, changed: { major, minor, patch, prerelease } } counting entries (downgrades count under their level).

dedupeReport(tree) -> entries for every package name that resolves to two or more DISTINCT versions anywhere in the tree: { name, versions: [{ version, paths }] }, entries sorted by name, versions by compareVersions ascending, paths sorted. Same version at several paths doesn't qualify on its own.

Contract is test_lockdiff.ts, run with `node --test test_lockdiff.ts`. Tests stay as they are.
